id.description: Unique identifier for the bundle installation.
Unique identifier for the project. An alphanumeric string that is 16 characters long.

ProjectShare.description: The share links of a project

time.description: Optional time component in 24-hour format (HH:MM:SS), e.g. "15:30:45". Can be null or omitted for date-only representations

timezone.description: Optional timezone identifier (IANA timezone name), e.g. "America/New_York", "Asia/Singapore". Can be null or omitted for timezone-naive representations

name.description: Human-readable name of the command in title case. This should probably be a verb.

description.description: Short summary for discovery (e.g. progressive disclosure / skill listing). The full instruction stays in `prompt`.

prompt.description: Tell the agent what this command will do. It should be positioned as a direct instruction to the agent. At least 30 words.

id.description: ID based on the name in snake case.

description.description: Role and purpose of agent, positioned as a direct instruction to the agent. Example: "You are a doctor that helps save lives.". At least 100 words.

value.description: Pick the most suitable emoji for this agent.

language.description: The language of the agent, e.g. en-US, zh-Hans

Get media with id
Download the binary content of a media file. Returns the file with the original Content-Type and Content-Disposition headers.
Download all media files from a space as a ZIP archive. Each file is stored as {mediaId}/metadata.json (id, kind, name, mimetype, size) and {mediaId}/original (binary content).

parameters.name: spaceId
parameters.description: The space (subspace / app) ID to download all media from.

post.description: Upload a media file to a space (root workspace or subspace / app). Send the file as the raw request body with the appropriate Content-Type header (e.g. image/png, application/pdf, application/octet-stream). Pass the original filename as the `filename` query parameter.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Original filename of the uploaded file (e.g. "photo.png").

parameters.name: spaceId
parameters.description: The space ID to upload media into (can be a root workspace or a subspace / app).

fileId.description: The file ID used in S3 (e.g. "space-files/uuid").

kind.description: Detected media kind (image, video, audio, document, etc.).

downloadUrl.description: Public URL to download the uploaded file.

get.description: Export a space (subspace / app) as a JSON bundle (SpaceBundleData v1). Returns all agents, automations, projects, templates, and apps. Media items are excluded — use the ZIP export or download media separately via the media endpoints.

parameters.name: spaceId
parameters.description: The space (subspace / app) ID to export.

items.description: Map of bundle items keyed by ID. Each item has a `type` field: space-bundle-flow-item, space-bundle-project-item, space-bundle-agent-item, space-bundle-template-item, space-bundle-media-item, space-bundle-app-item.

get.description: Export a space (subspace / app) as a ZIP bundle containing all agents, automations, projects, templates, apps, and media files. The ZIP includes a manifest.json, individual item files in type directories, and media files downloaded from storage.

parameters.name: spaceId
parameters.description: The space (subspace / app) ID to export.

post.description: Import a JSON bundle (agents, automations, projects, templates, apps) into a workspace. The bundle is validated against the SpaceBundleData v1 schema and all items are installed into the target workspace. Media items are not supported — use the ZIP import or upload media separately via the media endpoints after import.

items.description: Map of bundle items keyed by ID. Each item has a `type` field: space-bundle-flow-item, space-bundle-project-item, space-bundle-agent-item, space-bundle-template-item, space-bundle-media-item, space-bundle-app-item.

bundleData.description: The Workspace DNA bundle to import (SpaceBundleData v1).

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The target workspace ID to import the bundle into.

post.description: Import a ZIP/.tsk bundle (exported from the export/zip endpoint) into a workspace. All items are installed into the target workspace. The ZIP must contain valid bundle data (either bundle.json or directory structure). Media files in the ZIP are re-uploaded to storage automatically. Send the file as the raw request body with Content-Type: application/zip, application/octet-stream, or application/x-tsk.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The target workspace ID to import the bundle into.

get.description: Get public agent by public agent ID
